Title: DFT based analysis of pressure driven mechanical, opto-electronic, and thermoelectric properties in lead-free InGeX3 (X = Cl, Br) perovskites for solar energy applications
Abstract_Snippet: Lead halide perovskites have distinct physiochemical properties and demonstrate remarkable power conversion efficiency. InGeCl3 and InGeBr3 halide perovskite exhibit noteworthy changes in their electronic and optical properties under different pressure conditions. When the press...

Title: An approach towards next-generation hydrogen storage: a DFT study on A<sub>2</sub>LiTiH<sub>6</sub> (A = K, Ca) perovskite hydrides
Abstract_Snippet: This study explores the structural, mechanical, hydrogen storage, optical and thermodynamic properties of the double perovskite hydride A2LiTiH6 (A = K, Ca) by means of density functional theory (DFT). Title: Synergistic optoelectronic and thermoelectric performance in Rb2AsAuBr6 and Rb2AsAuCl6 double perovskites for multifunctional energy conversion
Abstract_Snippet: In this work, we use density functional theory (DFT) to thoroughly analyze the structural, electrical, optical, and thermoelectric characteristics of halide double perovskites Rb<sub>2</sub>AsAuX<sub>4</sub> (X = Br, Cl). Title: Optical properties of epitaxial SrHfO3 thin films grown on Si
Abstract_Snippet: The perovskite SrHfO3 can be a potential candidate among the high-permittivity materials for gate oxide replacement in future metal-oxide semiconductor field-effect transistor technology. Thin films of SrHfO3 were grown by molecular beam epitaxy and compared with SrTiO3 films. Title: Band gap tuning in ferroelectric Bi4Ti3O12 by alloying with LaTMO3 (TM = Ti, V, Cr, Mn, Co, Ni, and Al)
Abstract_Snippet: We fabricated ferroelectric Bi4Ti3O12 (BiT) single crystalline thin films site-specifically substituted with LaTMO3 (TM = Al, Ti, V, Cr, Mn, Co, and Ni) on SrTiO3 substrates by pulsed laser epitaxy. Abstract_Snippet: Ferroelectric oxides, which exhibit a spontaneous and reversible electric polarization, have recently gained interest for photovoltaic applications, because this polarization can potentially facilitate exciton separation and carrier extraction while also generating open-circuit voltages orders-of-magnitude larger than the band gap. Title: Spinelectronic investigation of the quaternary vanadium fluoride Rb2NaVF6: Ab-initio method
Abstract_Snippet: In this study, we have investigated the structural, electronic, and magnetic properties of the Rb2NaVF6 compound. Title: [Spectral analysis and band gap of RbVO3].

Title: First principles investigations of the electronic and magnetic properties of RVO3 (R = Er, Ho, Y, Lu) vanadates

Title: Thickness-Dependent Band Gap Modification in BaBiO3
Abstract_Snippet: The material BaBiO3 is known for its insulating character. However, for thin films, in the ultra-thin limit, metallicity is expected because the oxygen octahedra breathing mode will be suppressed as reported recently. Here, we confirm the influence of the oxygen breathing mode on the size of the band gap. Title: Optical absorption of NiPS<sub>3</sub>in the near-infrared, visible and near-ultraviolet regions
Abstract_Snippet: The optical absorption spectrum of NiPS3 in the energy range (0-5.4 eV) has been measured at 300, 78 and 4.2 K. The features observed in the spectrum have been interpreted in terms of transitions between the Ni2+ 3d levels and transitions between the S 3pxpy valence states and the Ni2+ 3d eg empty states. The Dq and B parameters have also been estimated.

Title: Structural investigation and thermodynamical properties of alkali calcium trihydrides
Abstract_Snippet: The ground-state structure, equilibrium structural parameters, electronic structure, and thermodynamical properties of MCaH3 (M=Li, Na, K, Rb, and Cs) phases have been investigated. Title: A dataset of DFT-computed oxygen vacancy formation energies of metal oxides

Title: A computational insight of the physical properties of MoSX (X= Cl, Br, I) for green energy applications

Title: Investigations of the structural, mechanical and optoelectronic attributes of Rb2BB'Tl I6 ( B'= As, Ga) double perovskites for photovoltaics

Title: First-principles calculations to investigate structural, electronic, optical, and magnetic properties of a scintillating double perovskite halide (Cs2LiCeCl6)
Abstract_Snippet: In this study, the investigations of structural, electronic, optical, and magnetic properties of a scintillating double perovskite halide (Cs2LiCeCl6) by using the full-potential linearized augmented plane wave (FP-LAPW) method in the framework of density functional theory (DFT) is reported. The optimized lattice constant (a0) is fully consistent with the experimental results, and the ground state optimized energy confirms the cubic (Fm 3¯ m space group) stability of the compound. Title: Appealing perspectives of the structural, electronic, elastic and optical properties of LiRCl<sub>3</sub> (R = Be and Mg) halide perovskites: a DFT study
Abstract_Snippet: To enhance the effectiveness of materials, we are motivated to investigate lithium-based halide perovskites LiRCl<sub>3</sub> (where R = Be and Mg) using first-principles techniques based on density functional theory (DFT), implemented in the WIEN2K code. In this study, the research makes use of the WIEN2K simulation code, employing the plane-wave and self-consistent (PWSCF) approach. Title: Revealing the Structural, Elastic, Electronic, and Optical Properties of K<sub>2</sub>ScCuCl<sub>6</sub> and K<sub>2</sub>YCuCl<sub>6</sub>: An In-Depth Exploration Using Density Functional Theory
Abstract_Snippet: The optoelectronic, structural, and elastic properties of K2ScCuCl6 and K2YCuCl6 double perovskite compounds were thoroughly investigated in this study using density functional theory. Title: Oriented tuning the photovoltaic properties ofγ-RbGeX3by strain-induced electron effective mass mutation
Abstract_Snippet: Based on the concept of green environmental protection, we hope to find a substitute for lead perovskites to reduce the pollution and achieve high photoelectric conversion efficiency. In this paper, the structural, electronic and optical properties of γ-RbGeX3 (X = Cl, Br and I) hybrid perovskites are investigated by density functional theory (DFT) calculations. Title: A dataset of DFT-computed oxygen vacancy formation energies of metal oxides
Abstract_Snippet: The oxygen vacancy formation energy ($ΔE_{vf}$) governs defect dynamics and is a useful metric to perform materials selection for a variety of applications. However, density functional theory (DFT) calculations of $ΔE_{vf}$ come at a greater computational cost than the typical bulk calculations available in materials databases due to the involvement of multiple vacancy-containing supercells. Title: A computational insight into Rb<sub>2</sub>ASbX<sub>6</sub> (A=Tl, Cu & X=I, Cl) double perovskites for energy storage and optoelectronic applications
Abstract_Snippet: Abstract Double perovskites are considered to be outstanding materials for encountering the energy crises faced by the world community. This article comprehensively addresses the electronic structure, mechanical, optical and transport attributes of Rb 2 ASbX 6 (A=Tl, Cu & X=I, Cl) double perovskites by using the DFT approach. Title: Computational Engineering of Direct-Bandgap Double Perovskites for Solar Cells
Abstract_Snippet: The pursuit of sustainable, lead-free perovskites has fueled the growing interest in halide double perovskites. This study utilizes first-principles calculations to explore the structural, elastic, electronic, and optical properties of Rb2NaInX6 (X=Br, I) and to evaluate their photovoltaic potential.
